text:
Once upon a time there was a lonely old tortoise. Every morning, she would wander slowly across the dew-covered grass. She had no friends aside from the squirrels who played occasionally. On sunny days, she would bask in the sun.

One day, while wandering through the thicket, she stumbled upon a mysterious old tree covered in moss. It glowed with an unearthly light. Curious, she shuffled closer. As she approached, the tree sang a song all its own—the sweetest melody she’d ever heard.

From its branches hung a hundred tiny jewels. Each one sparkled like tiny stars. She carefully gathered them into a nest she’d made years ago—a cozy nook lined with soft moss and petals.

Each night as she retired to her nest to sleep, the tree sang her lullabies. The owl at the edge of her grove would occasionally croon along.

Months passed, and the old tortoise grew curious about the source of the tree's songs
